name: Luxury Typography
summary: A premium editorial style led by refined type scale, generous spacing, disciplined copy rhythm, and restrained visual accents.

palette: quiet neutrals, deep ink, warm whites, and one refined accent
typography: large elegant headings with carefully set body copy
surfaces: minimal flat sections and premium editorial panels
shapes: simple frames, fine rules, and ample whitespace
layout: editorial hierarchy and deliberate negative space
imagery: high-quality product, portrait, venue, or material photography

avoid: Avoid exaggerated claims and cluttered value props.

recommendations: Use quiet neutral roles and a restrained accent.
Define display, heading, body, caption, and quote roles carefully.
avoid: Avoid large text without line-length control.

recommendations: Use minimal icons only when they clarify.
Use refined buttons with clear hit areas.
avoid: Avoid tiny luxury CTAs that harm usability.

recommendations: Use quiet cards with type-led hierarchy.
Use restrained nav and brand-forward spacing.
Use elegant legal and navigation structure.
Use headline, supporting copy, and fine divider rhythm.
Use clear command labels and refined borders.
Use underline, fine-rule, or subtle opacity changes.
Use refined fades and mask-like reveals.
Use low depth and editorial layering.
Use precise inline feedback with composed spacing.
avoid: Avoid casual microcopy for premium contexts.

accessibilityRisks.risk: Thin type and low contrast can harm readability.
accessibilityRisks.mitigation: Use accessible contrast and avoid thin body weights.

css: Use constrained line length, generous whitespace, and refined type scale.
do: Let typography create the premium signal.
avoid: Do not use tiny low-contrast controls.
